evidence.description: CORRECTION: My apologies; the below picture is of amputee models gathered for a photo shoot rather than wounded US veterans. The purpose of my original post was to bring more attention that many of our female warriors suffered physical and emotional harm. That remains my intent and focus. Two organizations I support that help our brave men and women are: Wounded Warrior Project: https://lnkd.in/gztyfPmD Folds of Honor: https://foldsofhonor.org Earlier this year, I read many of our service members were struggling with household expenses due to our double-digit percent inflation. It was suggested by our government that they should apply for food stamps. That recommendation shocked and angered me. Thousands of our military men and women paid a price for our freedoms. They did it for wages barely above minimum wage. Thank you to all who serve! “These ladies pictured participated in a project to help people who, like them, need to use prostheses after having amputated body members but do not have financial conditions to cover the costs of the mechanism. The photos were used to create a calendar, launched in 2016.” I should have researched the post before reposting it. Thank you for bringing this to my attention @TimTurner | 276 comments on LinkedIn
evidence.text:
CORRECTION: My apologies; the below picture is of amputee models gathered for a photo shoot rather than wounded US veterans. The purpose of my original post was to bring more attention that many of our female warriors suffered physical and emotional harm. That remains my intent and focus. Two organizations I support that help our brave men and women are: Wounded Warrior Project: https://lnkd.in/gztyfPmD Folds of Honor: https://foldsofhonor.org Earlier this year, I read many of our service members were struggling with household expenses due to our double-digit percent inflation. It was suggested by our government that they should apply for food stamps. That recommendation shocked and angered me. Thousands of our military men and women paid a price for our freedoms. They did it for wages barely above minimum wage. Thank you to all who serve! “These ladies pictured participated in a project to help people who, like them, need to use prostheses after having amputated body members but do not have financial conditions to cover the costs of the mechanism. The photos were used to create a calendar, launched in 2016.” I should have researched the post before reposting it. Thank you for bringing this to my attention @TimTurner
Amazing!!!!!!
May God bless you all
Awesome girls just awesome. God Bless you.
You're all unsung heroes!!!
I can telll they have the warrior spirit. Good for them. No victims in that photo.
1% of our population serves to protect 300M+ people and yet we can’t properly take care of our veterans and their families - food stamps? Speaking of numbers the DoD passed a budget for $780B … that’s a lot of millions.
Thank you🙏❤️🇺🇸
Every one of these ladies are simply lovely
It’s the right thing to do!
Styron Powers, Harvard Advanced Management, Rutgers MBA , thank you for correcting your post. You didn’t have to but you did. Integrity first!

Vithia Monica lee liked thisVithia Monica lee liked thisWe've all experienced the heat that electronics can produce. Just think of the heat data centers create with servers running 24-7! We've got a solution for that - CompuZol™ Immersion Fluid Solution by Lubrizol. CompuZol comes in direct contact with the heat-generating servers, transferring heat much more efficiently than traditional air-cooled systems. This reduces data center cooling costs by an estimated 30%! Better yet, CompuZol™ requires less water than traditional air-cooling systems and reduces reliance on air conditioners to cool the space. A great sustainability win! The innovative features don't stop there. Learn more here: https://lnkd.in/gAvG8qeJ #TechTuesday #Innovation

Vithia Monica lee liked thisVithia Monica lee liked thisThe power of being authentic. Embracing and staying true to your authentic self creates an environment where others feel encouraged to do the same. Here's why it matters: Inspiring others ❤️: When people witness someone embracing their genuine self, it serves as inspiration for them to follow suit. By being authentic, you give others the courage to let go of facades and societal pressures, empowering them to express their true thoughts, emotions, and identities. Building trust and connections 👍: Authenticity fosters trust and cultivates stronger connections. When you are open and honest about who you are, people are more likely to trust you and form deeper bonds. Authenticity nurtures genuine interactions and lays the foundation for meaningful relationships. Creating a safe space 🙏: Authentic self-expression creates a safe and accepting space for others to do the same. By being non-judgmental and open, you encourage others to share their true selves without fear of rejection or criticism. Promoting diversity and inclusion 👏: Embracing authenticity celebrates diversity and promotes inclusivity. When individuals feel empowered to be their genuine selves, it allows for a broader range of perspectives, experiences, and talents to be recognized and valued. Embracing your authenticity not only benefits you but also has a positive ripple effect on those around you. It inspires others to be more genuine, deepens connections, and contributes to a more inclusive and accepting society. Let your authenticity shine and create a space where everyone can thrive. I started a newsletter about authentic leadership, feel free to subscribe to it here: Maybe you can’t control everything, but you can always improve. If you deeply care about improving your customer service, feel free to subscribe to my newsletter here: https://lnkd.in/ejGrHJdN #professionalwomen #motivation #leadership #diversityandinclusion

Vithia Monica lee liked thisVithia Monica lee liked thisThis month was #NationalVolunteerMonth and we are taking a look back at some of the amazing volunteer efforts from our employees. Recently, employees from our Cincinnati, OH site volunteered with their local Adopt-A-Spot program where they dedicated an hour to clean up litter and removed approximately 18 bags of trash. Employees from our Salt Lake City, UT site volunteered at their local food bank sorting donations and filling boxes with food that go out into the community. Additionally, employees from our Parsippany, NJ HQ volunteered with Heart to Heart International to assemble approximately 1800 hygiene kits for disaster relief and/or communities in need. Thank you to everyone who volunteered their time this month and for all your efforts! Learn more about #LifeatTeva here: https://bit.ly/3Nfbv63 #TevaUSA #Volunteering #TevaGives

Audits rarely fail because of one big issue. They fail because of repeatable patterns. Pass Your First GMP Audit in QC Nail the basics. Show control. Prove data integrity. 🚀 Your 7-step playbook Prep: Current SOPs, training matrix, clear RACI. Records: ALCOA+ docs, real-time entries, version control. Methods & Equipment: Validations, IQ/OQ/PQ, calibration logs, standards traceability. Digital transformation in GMP laboratories is no longer optional. It is a regulatory expectation and a competitive advantage. As data volumes grow and manual processes strain compliance, regulators expect strong data integrity controls, real-time visibility, and risk-based decision making. This session delivers a practical roadmap to modernize GMP labs while staying inspection ready. Through real case studies, we explore ALCOA+, audit trails, system validation, cybersecurity, cloud governance, predictive maintenance, and digital dashboards aligned with FDA and EU GMP expectations. Learn how digitally mature laboratories move from reactive compliance to proactive inspection readiness, reducing deviations, accelerating investigations, and strengthening regulatory confidence. https://lnkd.in/e3x32PQ3

Prodigy BPC
89 followers
Most quality systems don’t fail in audits. They fail in operations. They fail when: • A handoff breaks • A decision isn’t traceable • A deviation takes weeks to untangle • Or evidence lives in someone’s head That’s why regulatory changes like QMSR hit so hard. They don’t expose documentation gaps. They expose how the business actually runs. The uncomfortable truth: your real quality system is your operating model. Prodigy BPC helps organizations make operations defensible, controls real, and evidence automatic — not dependent on heroics or tribal knowledge. If your confidence in quality depends on specific people being in the room, that’s not a system. That’s risk. #Compliance #FDA #RiskManagement #Quality #QMR #QMSR #OperationalQuality #QualitySystem

The best audit prep isn’t a checklist—it’s a culture. When sampling, monitoring, and verification are part of your daily process, audit readiness becomes second nature. Be ready for every inspection—because your data already tells the story. Learn more at https://lnkd.in/gRUbE4DD. #AsepticSampling #QualityManagement #AuditReady #FoodSafety

Nexus Metrology
176 followers
Most GMP sites think their calibration program is “fine.” Until an audit exposes one hard question. • How are decision rules applied? • How was calibration frequency justified? • Who owns metrology risk across R&D and Manufacturing? • What happens when your SME leaves? Calibration ≠ Compliance. And “it’s calibrated” does not equal “audit ready.” At Nexus | Metrology, we step in as a Fractional Head of Metrology for biotech and pharma sites that: ✔ Are scaling fast ✔ Are preparing for inspection ✔ Lost internal metrology leadership ✔ Or simply don’t have time to assess risk properly We don’t just perform calibrations. We own the program. From risk ranking to SOP structure, vendor oversight, ISO 17025 alignment, and FDA audit defense, we build systems that survive scrutiny. If your site is: Too small for a full-time Manager Too critical to operate without one Let’s talk. www.nexuscmm.com 👈 #GMP #PharmaceuticalManufacturing #Biotech #Calibration #Metrology #FDA #AuditReady #AssetManagement #QualitySystems
